title: Notification on a Date Change
excerpt:
My company is new to Monday.com so I apologize if I missed something. 
We ship product to our study site based upon the date they give us as the study procedure date.  This date can be changed several times by the study …

posts.author: jsdelapasse (Scott Delapasse)
posts.cookedHtml:
My company is new to Monday.com so I apologize if I missed something.

We ship product to our study site based upon the date they give us as the study procedure date. This date can be changed several times by the study site.

There doesn’t appear to be an automation that would do something like:

When DATE changes (or modified) NOTIFY SOMEONE.

If there is a way to do this, I surely missed it.

BTW, I did try to create this using the custom automation builder, using the format:
When COLUMN changes NOTIFY SOMEONE
I then chose the Date column for the COLUMN placehoder in the formula. Unfortunately this triggered a notification when ANY colum was changed. If I change COLUMN to DATE in the custom builder - there is no option to trigger off of a change in date.

Any suggestions

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jsdelapasse - thanks for joining the monday.com community

We do offer an automation to send a notification based on a column change under the Notifications section of the automation center:

image726×542 20.1 KB

This sounds very similar to what you built out using the custom automations builder! This automation allows you to define a column as the Date column on your board. Then whenever any item’s date is changed on your board, whoever you have selected in the someone field will receive a notification in their bell icon. They can also receive these notifications via email depending on their notification preferences.

Does this help to answer your question?

posts.author: jsdelapasse (Scott Delapasse)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have absolutely no idea how I missed that!

BTW – I did create that same automation using the CUSTOM RECIPE. It did send the notification, however, it sent the notification on EVERY column change, not just the column I specified.

Thank you and sorry for any inconvenience….

posts.author: rosovconsulting (Rosov Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a way to notify a person ONLY if they are assigned to the task?

In other words, if I use this automation, when DATE column changes, notify ME, then I will be notified every single time the dates are changed on all tasks on a board. I would like to be notified ONLY when I (or a team I am on) is assigned to that task. Is that possible? Is there a different way to restrict this notification?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rosovconsulting, good question! Have you already tried using custom automations for this? I would recommend the following recipe:

image1302×193 15.1 KB

You can set it up to notify you any time the date column (or any other column) changes:

Build your own custom automation

posts.author: rosovconsulting (Rosov Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks - I had not noticed the “and” operator.

I just tried setting this up, but I could only select a “user” for “Person is assigned to …”, not a “team” (although I could select a team for “notify someone…”). Am I missing something? If not, it would be great to add this feature.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rosovconsulting, good spot!

It looks like the “assign team” option is only available in an end block:

image733×304 15.7 KB

Could this work for you at all? Will pass your feedback along!

posts.author: rosovconsulting (Rosov Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t think so. I’m not looking to “assign a team”. The team is already assigned to the item. I want the notification to send for an item already assigned to a team (instead of just a single person):

image1290×193 16.3 KB

So “someone” should be “Production Team”, since they are assigned to tasks:

image979×49 7.44 KB

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it, thanks for explaining! I’m afraid we don’t have this functionality available at the moment—the way to go for now would be to set up multiple automations (one for each person).

posts.author: rosovconsulting (Rosov Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: If a task is assigned to a “team”, would the automation trigger if I just selected an individual person in the “assigned to” field? Or will it not recognize the person, since the task is not assigned to that person individually?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @rosovconsulting! In that case it would have to be assigned to a person individually to trigger.

posts.author: Reinder (Reinder Roos)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, related to the date change question - is there a way to only notify people when a timeline is moved for > # days? I do not want to annoy users with every minor change in timing.

posts.author: Hcurtis (Henrietta Curtis)
posts.cookedHtml: Did you ever get an answer to this?
